what_you_learned: Phishing emails use generic greetings like 'Dear Customer'
Fake sender domains look close but are never the real company domain
Urgency and threat of account deletion are manipulation signals
Urgency language like '2 HOURS' is a FOMO tactic to bypass rational thinking
Real companies give you time — attackers never do
Pause and verify through official channels before clicking anything
Authority bias makes us comply with executives without questioning
CEO fraud requests always bypass normal approval processes
Any request for secrecy is an immediate red flag
Always hover over links before clicking to see the real destination
Lookalike domains swap one letter or use a different TLD
Display text can say anything — the URL is what matters
Executable files (.exe, .bat, .ps1) should never arrive via email
Even .docx and .pdf can carry malicious macros — enable macros only when expected
Unexpected attachments from unknown senders are always suspicious
You can identify all major phishing attack vectors
Slow down and verify — your best defence is a second of thought
Real urgency never comes via email
